title: Dow Jones| Nasdaq | S&P 500 | US Stock Market Today | Live: US stock futures decline as US-Iran escalation rattles sentiment - The Economic Times
text:
Dow Jones Today | S&P 500 | US Stock Market Live: Wall Street futures fell as renewed US-Iran tensions in the Gulf pushed oil prices higher and pressured chip stocks. Nasdaq futures led losses, while investors braced for key earnings and inflation data that could shape rate expectations.

Bank of America said on Monday it has hired nine senior investment bankers across the United States as it looks to strengthen its regional investment banking presence and tap rising demand from middle-market companies.

US President Donald Trump said the United States could take over control of the Strait of Hormuz and position itself as the “guardian” of the strategically critical waterway. Speaking in a phone interview with Fox News’ “Fox & Friends,” Trump said Washington would likely “run” the strait to ensure its security, given its importance for global oil shipments. He added that the US should be compensated for maintaining control and safeguarding the passage, signalling a potential shift in approach towards one of the world’s most vital energy transit routes.
